name: Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar — circular route
name_original: Muros de Nalón a Playa Aguilar — ruta circular
description: Jog this 5.7-mile (9.1 km) moderate circular route from Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar, featuring stunning coastal views and varied terrain
region_intro: Jogging around Muros De Nalón offers diverse routes along the Asturian coast, characterized by its rugged cliffs, sandy beaches, and the Nalón River estuary. The region features well-maintained coastal paths, sections through pine and eucalyptus forests, and varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. These running routes provide continuous views of the Cantabrian Sea and access to natural features. Best jogging routes around Muros De Nalón The most popular jogging route is Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar — circular route, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal vistas and passes by Playa de Aguilar. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador de Monteagudo loop from Muros de Nalón, a difficult 12.3 miles (19.8 km) path. This route provides expansive views from Mirador de Monteagudo and traverses varied landscapes. Local runners also love the Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and local forests, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Jogging around Muros De Nalón is defined by coastal paths, impressive cliffs, and sections through pine and eucalyptus forests. The network offers options for both easy runs and more challenging trails. The routes in Muros De Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Muros De Nalón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Muros De Nalón?
A: Muros De Nalón offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 50 trails to explore. These range from easy coastal paths to more challenging inland loops, ensuring options for various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Muros De Nalón?
A: Running in Muros De Nalón provides a diverse array of stunning scenery. You'll encounter breathtaking coastal views along rugged cliffs, pass by beautiful sandy beaches like Playa de Aguilar, and traverse sections through peaceful pine and eucalyptus forests. The region also features the natural beauty of the Nalón River estuary.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, Muros De Nalón has options for all fitness levels. While many routes are moderate, there are at least 2 easy running trails available. These typically feature well-maintained paths and gentler inclines, perfect for a relaxed jog or for beginners.

Q: Can you tell me more about the Ruta de los Miradores for running?
A: The Ruta de los Miradores, or Path of the Viewpoints, is a prominent coastal path ideal for running. It offers spectacular views of the Cantabrian Sea and impressive cliffs. Along this route, you'll find several viewpoints like Mirador del Espíritu Santo and Mirador de la Atalaya, providing continuous scenic vistas. The path is well-signposted and features varied terrain, including cobbled sections, forest paths, and sandy areas near beaches.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Muros De Nalón?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Muros De Nalón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056075254/muros-de-nalon-to-playa-aguilar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt; also offers a great circular experience through coastal areas and forests.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Muros De Nalón?
A: The running routes in Muros De Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable running experience. The combination of sea, beaches, and forests is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest I can see along the running routes?
A: Yes, several interesting landmarks and natural features can be enjoyed. Along coastal routes, you'll pass beautiful beaches like Playa de Aguilar and Playa de Xilo, and numerous viewpoints offering panoramic sea views. Nearby attractions include historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt;, as well as impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2005855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Muros De Nalón suitable for families?
A: Many of the trails, especially the easier and moderate coastal paths, are suitable for families. The well-signposted Ruta de los Miradores, with its rest areas and accessible paths, is a great option for a family jog or walk, allowing everyone to enjoy the beautiful scenery.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes in Muros De Nalón?
A: While specific parking details for every route vary, Muros De Nalón is generally well-equipped for visitors. You can typically find parking in the main town areas or near popular starting points for trails, especially those leading to beaches or viewpoints. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Muros De Nalón?
A: Muros De Nalón offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally provide mild temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, especially midday. The coastal paths offer refreshing sea breezes during warmer periods.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24581165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Monteagudo loop from Muros de Nalón&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 12.3-mile (19.8 km) path with significant elevation changes. This route provides expansive views and traverses varied, demanding landscapes.

Q: Can I find running routes that connect to the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Muros De Nalón is part of the Camino del Norte (Northern Way of St. James). This offers a unique opportunity to incorporate sections of this historic pilgrimage route into your run, providing a distinct experience and potentially longer jogging options through a privileged natural environment.
tips: A small coastal municipality of just over 1,800 inhabitants. It is worth seeing the stately town of Muros (of Roman and medieval origin), the walls of the old Palace of Valdecarzana and the Palace of the Marqués de Muros, and its old mansions and beautiful buildings.
The presence of enormous, beautiful rocks on the coast gives this beach, the most occupied in the municipality of Muros de Nalón, a great attraction.
A very nice viewpoint on the road that goes down to the Aguilar beach. A luxury that deserves to stop to enjoy.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar circular route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Muros de Nalón, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas may be available near the trailhead or within the town itself.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: While generally considered 'moderate' for jogging, this route includes a significant initial ascent of approximately 415 to 420 steps near San Esteban. This makes it a more demanding start for runners, so a good level of fitness is recommended for beginner joggers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, including 'senda empedrada' (cobbled or stone path), 'firme de tierra' (dirt path), and some sections with concrete or mostly paved surfaces. Be prepared for the initial long staircase.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter breathtaking coastal views, often described as 'magníficas vistas de acantilados' (magnificent views of cliffs). Key highlights include Muros de Nalón, Playa de Aguilar, and the View of Aguilar Beach. Other notable spots are the Mirador del Espíritu Santo and Mirador de L'Atalaya, offering panoramic vistas over the Cantabrian Sea and the Nalón River mouth.

faqs.question: Is the Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, near cliffs, or when encountering other trail users and wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Asturian coast is beautiful year-round, but spring and early autumn often offer the most pleasant weather for jogging, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or heavy rain. Summer can be popular, but also warmer.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Muros de Nalón to Playa Aguilar circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or water sources available on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the path is equipped with recreational areas and water fountains, making it suitable for longer outings. Playa de Aguilar also has good facilities as a Blue Flag beach.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Northern Route (Asturia), Mtb Northern Route (Asturia), and various segments of the Paseo de los Miradores.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route typically takes around 1 hour and 2 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 9.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: Are there any hidden beaches or coves accessible from the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the path winds past several hidden beaches and coves such as La Atalaya, La Cazonera, Las Llanas, Veneiro, and Xilo. While accessible, some descents to these beaches can be challenging.
